1663.iwh_en: Abdullah ibn Salam (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'A group of us companions of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ sat together and discussed: if we only knew which deeds are most beloved to Allah, He would tell us — so Allah revealed: {Whatever is in the heavens and on earth glorifies Allah...} to the end of the surah.'
1663.iwh_id: Abdullah ibn Salam (radhiyallahu 'anhu) berkata: 'Sekelompok dari kami sahabat Rasulullah ﷺ duduk bersama dan berdiskusi: seandainya kami tahu amalan mana yang paling dicintai Allah, niscaya Dia memberitahu kami — maka Allah menurunkan: {Segala yang di langit dan di bumi bertasbih kepada Allah...} hingga akhir surah.'

1664.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Allah has guaranteed the one who goes out from his home for nothing but jihad in His cause and belief in His words, that He will either admit him into Paradise or return him to his home with the reward and spoils he has gained.'
1664.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Allah menjamin orang yang keluar dari rumahnya semata-mata karena jihad di jalan-Nya dan keyakinan terhadap firman-Nya, bahwa Dia akan memasukkannya ke surga atau mengembalikannya ke rumahnya dengan pahala dan rampasan yang diperolehnya.'

1665.iwh_en: It was narrated from Jabir (may Allah be pleased with him) that someone asked: O Messenger of Allah, which jihad is most virtuous? He said: 'The one whose horse is hamstrung and whose blood is shed.'
1665.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Jabir (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seseorang bertanya: Wahai Rasulullah, jihad mana yang paling utama? Beliau bersabda: 'Orang yang kudanya dipotong ototnya dan darahnya tertumpah.'

1666.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ was asked: Which deeds are most virtuous? He said: 'Faith in Allah and His Messenger.' It was asked: Then what? He said: 'Jihad in the cause of Allah.' It was asked: Then what? He said: 'An accepted Hajj.'
1666.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ ditanya: Amalan mana yang paling utama? Beliau bersabda: 'Iman kepada Allah dan Rasul-Nya.' Ditanyakan: Kemudian apa? Beliau bersabda: 'Jihad di jalan Allah.' Ditanyakan: Kemudian apa? Beliau bersabda: 'Haji mabrur.'

1667.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mu'adh ibn Jabal (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever fights in the cause of Allah for even the interval between two milkings of a she-camel, Paradise becomes obligatory for him.'
1667.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mu'adh ibn Jabal (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa berperang di jalan Allah meskipun sekadar jeda antara dua kali pemerahan unta betina, surga menjadi wajib baginya.'

1668.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Abbas (may Allah be pleased with them) that the Prophet ﷺ came out to them while they were sitting and said: 'Should I not tell you of the best of people in this station?' They said: Yes. He said: 'A man who takes his horse's reins in the cause of Allah, ready whenever the call comes...'
1668.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Abbas (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Nabi ﷺ keluar menemui mereka yang sedang duduk dan bersabda: 'Maukah kalian aku beritahu tentang sebaik-baik manusia di posisi ini?' Mereka berkata: Ya. Beliau bersabda: 'Seorang laki-laki yang memegang tali kekang kudanya di jalan Allah, siap kapan pun seruan datang...'

1669.iwh_en: It was narrated from Imran ibn Husayn (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A man's standing in a battle line in the cause of Allah is better than sixty years of worship.'
1669.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Imran ibn Husain (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Seorang laki-laki berdiri dalam barisan perang di jalan Allah lebih baik daripada enam puluh tahun ibadah.'

1670.iwh_en: It was narrated that Malik ibn Abdullah passed by Habib ibn Maslamah (or Habib passed by Malik) while he was leading a horse and walking. He said: 'Why do you not ride?' He said: 'I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: whoever walks a step in the cause of Allah, Allah raises him one degree and erases from him one sin.'
1670.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan bahwa Malik ibn Abdullah melewati Habib ibn Maslamah (atau Habib melewati Malik) sementara dia menuntun kuda dan berjalan kaki. Dia berkata: 'Mengapa engkau tidak menunggang?' Dia berkata: 'Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: barangsiapa berjalan selangkah di jalan Allah, Allah mengangkatnya satu derajat dan menghapus darinya satu dosa.'

1671.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sahl ibn Sa'd (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A morning expedition in the cause of Allah or an evening expedition in the cause of Allah is better than the world and all it contains.'
1671.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Sahl ibn Sa'd (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Satu ekspedisi pagi hari di jalan Allah atau satu ekspedisi sore hari di jalan Allah lebih baik daripada dunia dan segala isinya.'

1672.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (may Allah be pleased with him) from the Prophet ﷺ who said: 'There is no servant who fasts one day in the cause of Allah except that Allah will distance his face from the Fire by seventy years because of that day.'
1672.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Sa'id al-Khudri (radhiyallahu 'anhu) dari Nabi ﷺ yang bersabda: 'Tidaklah seorang hamba berpuasa satu hari di jalan Allah melainkan Allah akan menjauhkan wajahnya dari api neraka sejauh perjalanan tujuh puluh tahun karena puasa hari itu.'

1673.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Rayhanah (may Allah be pleased with him) that he was with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ on a campaign, and the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Hellfire is forbidden for every eye that weeps out of fear of Allah, and every eye that kept vigil in the cause of Allah.'
1673.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Raihanah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia bersama Rasulullah ﷺ dalam suatu ekspedisi, dan Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Neraka diharamkan bagi setiap mata yang menangis karena takut kepada Allah, dan setiap mata yang berjaga di jalan Allah.'

1674.iwh_en: It was narrated from Uqbah ibn Amir al-Juhani (may Allah be pleased with him) from the Prophet ﷺ who said: 'May Allah have mercy on the one standing guard, the one standing watch, and the one on post — what a great reward they shall have!'
1674.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Uqbah ibn Amir al-Juhani (radhiyallahu 'anhu) dari Nabi ﷺ yang bersabda: 'Semoga Allah merahmati orang yang berjaga, orang yang berjaga malam, dan orang yang bertugas pos — sungguh besar pahala yang akan mereka dapatkan!'

1675.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Mas'ud al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man came with a bridled she-camel and said: This is in the cause of Allah. The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'You will have for it on the Day of Resurrection seven hundred she-camels, all bridled.'
1675.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Mas'ud al-Ansari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki datang membawa unta betina yang sudah diikatkan talinya dan berkata: Ini untuk jalan Allah. Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Kamu akan mendapatkan tujuh ratus unta betina di hari kiamat, semuanya sudah diikatkan talinya.'

1676.iwh_en: It was narrated that Sa'sa'ah ibn Mu'awiyah said: I met Abu Dharr (may Allah be pleased with him) while he was driving or leading a camel with a waterskin around its neck. I said: O Abu Dharr, what wealth do you have? He said: My deeds are my wealth. He said: He would give away whatever he acquired, and when asked: What wealth do you have? He replied: My deeds in front of me.
1676.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan bahwa Sa'sa'ah ibn Mu'awiyah berkata: Aku bertemu Abu Dzar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) sementara dia menggiring atau menuntun unta dengan kantong air di lehernya. Aku berkata: Wahai Abu Dzar, apa kekayaanmu? Dia berkata: Amalku adalah kekayaanku. Dia berkata: Dia selalu memberikan apa yang diperolehnya, dan ketika ditanya: Apa kekayaanmu? Dia menjawab: Amalku di hadapanku.

1677.iwh_en: It was narrated from Uqbah ibn Amir (may Allah be pleased with him) that he recited this verse: {And prepare against them whatever force and horses you can} — he said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: 'Know that force is archery. Know that force is archery. Know that force is archery.'
1677.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Uqbah ibn Amir (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia membaca ayat ini: {Dan siapkanlah untuk menghadapi mereka kekuatan apa saja yang kamu sanggupi dan kuda-kuda yang ditambatkan} — dia berkata: Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Ketahuilah bahwa kekuatan itu adalah memanah. Ketahuilah bahwa kekuatan itu adalah memanah. Ketahuilah bahwa kekuatan itu adalah memanah.'

1678.iwh_en: It was narrated from Uqbah ibn Amir (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Verily Allah admits three people into Paradise with one arrow: the maker who makes it hoping for good, the one who shoots it, and the one who retrieves it. So practice archery and riding. That you practice archery is more beloved to me than that you ride.'
1678.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Uqbah ibn Amir (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Sesungguhnya Allah memasukkan tiga orang ke surga dengan satu anak panah: pembuatnya yang membuatnya dengan harapan kebaikan, orang yang memanahnya, dan orang yang mengambilkannya. Maka berlatihlah memanah dan berkuda. Engkau berlatih memanah lebih aku sukai daripada engkau berkuda.'

1679.iwh_en: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Shoot and ride. That you shoot is more beloved to me than that you ride.'
1679.iwh_id: Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Berlatihlah memanah dan berkuda. Engkau memanah lebih aku sukai daripada engkau berkuda.'

1680.iwh_en: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Everything with which a man amuses himself is vain except a man's shooting with his bow, his training of his horse, and his play with his wife — for these are from the truth.'
1680.iwh_id: Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Segala sesuatu yang menjadi hiburan bagi seorang laki-laki adalah sia-sia kecuali memanah dengan busurnya, melatih kudanya, dan bercengkerama dengan istrinya — karena ini semua termasuk yang hak.'

1681.iwh_en: The Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever abandons archery after having learned it has indeed been ungrateful for what he was taught.'
1681.iwh_id: Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa meninggalkan memanah setelah mempelajarinya, sungguh dia telah tidak mensyukuri apa yang telah diajarkan kepadanya.'

1682.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'There is no one wounded in the cause of Allah — and Allah knows best who is truly wounded in His cause — but that he will come on the Day of Resurrection with his wound gushing blood; its color will be the color of blood but its scent will be the scent of musk.'
1682.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Tidaklah seseorang yang terluka di jalan Allah — dan Allah lebih mengetahui siapa yang benar-benar terluka di jalan-Nya — melainkan dia akan datang pada Hari Kiamat dengan lukanya yang mengucurkan darah; warnanya adalah warna darah namun aromanya adalah aroma musk.'

1683.iwh_en: It was narrated from Sahl ibn Hunaif (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever sincerely asks Allah for martyrdom, Allah will grant him the rank of the martyrs even if he dies in his bed.'
1683.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Sahl ibn Hunaif (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa berdoa dengan tulus kepada Allah meminta syahid, Allah akan menganugerahinya derajat para syuhada meskipun dia meninggal di atas tempat tidurnya.'

1684.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Hurairah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A martyr does not feel the pain of killing except as one of you feels the pain of a pinch.'
1684.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Hurairah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Seorang syahid tidak merasakan sakit terbunuh kecuali seperti salah seorang di antara kalian merasakan sakit cubitan.'

1685.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'No soul that dies and enters Paradise would wish to return to you even if it had the world and all it contains — except the martyr, who will wish to return to the world and be killed ten times because of the honor he sees.'
1685.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Tidak ada jiwa yang meninggal lalu masuk surga yang ingin kembali kepada kalian meskipun memiliki dunia dan segala isinya — kecuali syahid, yang ingin kembali ke dunia dan terbunuh sepuluh kali karena kemuliaan yang dia lihat.'

1686.iwh_en: Masruq (may Allah have mercy on him) said: We asked Abdullah (ibn Mas'ud) about the souls of the martyrs, and were it not for him no one would have narrated to us. He said: 'The souls of the martyrs are with Allah on the Day of Resurrection in the bellies of green birds; they have lanterns hanging from the Throne, and they roam freely in Paradise wherever they wish, then return to their lanterns.'
1686.iwh_id: Masruq (rahimahullah) berkata: Kami bertanya kepada Abdullah (ibn Mas'ud) tentang ruh para syuhada, dan kalau bukan karena dia tidak ada yang akan menceritakan kepada kami. Dia berkata: 'Ruh para syuhada berada di sisi Allah pada Hari Kiamat dalam perut burung-burung hijau; mereka memiliki lentera-lentera yang tergantung dari 'Arsy, dan mereka berkeliaran bebas di surga ke mana pun mereka inginkan, kemudian kembali ke lentera-lentera mereka.'

1687.iwh_en: It was narrated from Utbah ibn Abd al-Sulami (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The slain are three: a man who believed and fought in the cause of Allah with his soul and wealth until he met the enemy and fought until he was killed — that is the tested martyr in Allah's tent under His Throne; prophets are not above him except by virtue of prophethood...'
1687.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Utbah ibn Abd al-Sulami (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Yang terbunuh ada tiga: seorang laki-laki yang beriman dan berperang di jalan Allah dengan jiwa dan hartanya hingga bertemu musuh dan berperang hingga terbunuh — itulah syahid yang teruji di kemah Allah di bawah 'Arsy-Nya; para nabi tidak berada di atasnya kecuali karena kenabian...'

1688.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Qatadah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ stood and delivered a sermon, praising Allah and extolling Him, then mentioned jihad and said: 'The best thing for people to do after themselves is: a horse for the cause of Allah, a camel for the cause of Allah, a cow for the cause of Allah.'
1688.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Qatadah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ berdiri dan berkhutbah, memuji Allah dan menyanjung-Nya, kemudian menyebut jihad dan bersabda: 'Sebaik-baik yang bisa dilakukan manusia setelah diri mereka sendiri adalah: kuda di jalan Allah, unta di jalan Allah, sapi di jalan Allah.'

1689.iwh_en: It was narrated from Safwan ibn Umayyah (may Allah be pleased with him) from the Prophet ﷺ who said: 'Plague is martyrdom, drowning is martyrdom, campaigning is martyrdom, and a woman who dies in childbirth — her child drags her by the umbilical cord into Paradise.'
1689.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Safwan ibn Umayyah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) dari Nabi ﷺ yang bersabda: 'Wabah tha'un adalah syahid, tenggelam adalah syahid, berperang adalah syahid, dan wanita yang meninggal karena melahirkan — anaknya akan menariknya dengan tali pusarnya masuk surga.'

1690.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ubadah ibn al-Samit (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Being killed in the cause of Allah is martyrdom, and plague is martyrdom, and drowning is martyrdom, and stomach ailment is martyrdom, and the woman who dies with her newborn is dragged by it to Paradise.'
1690.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ubadah ibn al-Samit (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Terbunuh di jalan Allah adalah syahid, wabah tha'un adalah syahid, tenggelam adalah syahid, sakit perut adalah syahid, dan wanita yang meninggal bersama bayi yang baru lahirnya akan ditarik olehnya ke surga.'

1691.iwh_en: Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqas (may Allah be pleased with him) said: 'We used to campaign with the Messenger of Allah ﷺ having no food except for this samur tree and the leaves of the hubla plant, until one of us would excrete as a sheep excretes, having no covering for his thighs.'
1691.iwh_id: Sa'd ibn Abi Waqqash (radhiyallahu 'anhu) berkata: 'Kami dahulu berperang bersama Rasulullah ﷺ tanpa memiliki makanan selain pohon samur ini dan daun-daun pohon hubla, hingga salah seorang dari kami buang hajat seperti kambing buang hajat, tidak memiliki penutup untuk kedua pahanya.'

1692.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ubadah ibn al-Samit (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Whoever campaigns in the cause of Allah and does not intend by his campaign except in exchange for a camel's halter cord, then he will have only what he intended.'
1692.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ubadah ibn al-Samit (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa berperang di jalan Allah dan tidak berniat dengan peperangannya kecuali untuk mendapatkan tali kekang unta, maka dia hanya akan mendapatkan apa yang dia niatkan.'

1693.iwh_en: It was narrated from Mu'adh ibn Jabal (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Campaigning is of two types: as for the one who seeks Allah's face, obeys the commander, gives generously of his wealth, treats his companions well, and avoids corruption — his entire sleep and wakefulness is a reward. And as for the one who campaigns for pride and fame, who disobeys the commander and causes corruption — he does not return equal.'
1693.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Mu'adh ibn Jabal (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Berperang ada dua macam: adapun orang yang mencari ridha Allah, mematuhi komandan, dermawan dengan hartanya, baik kepada rekan-rekannya, dan menghindari kerusakan — seluruh tidur dan terjaganya adalah pahala. Dan adapun orang yang berperang karena kebanggaan dan ketenaran, yang tidak mematuhi komandan dan menyebabkan kerusakan — dia tidak kembali dalam keadaan setara.'

1694.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Umamah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Prophet ﷺ said: 'Whoever does not campaign, does not equip a campaigner, or does not take good care of a campaigner's family in his absence — Allah will afflict him with a calamity before the Day of Resurrection.'
1694.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Umamah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda: 'Barangsiapa tidak berperang, tidak membekali orang yang berperang, atau tidak merawat dengan baik keluarga orang yang berperang saat dia pergi — Allah akan menimpakan bencana kepadanya sebelum Hari Kiamat.'

1695.iwh_en: It was narrated from Zayd ibn Khalid al-Juhani (may Allah be pleased with him) from the Prophet ﷺ who said: 'Whoever equips a warrior in the cause of Allah or looks after his family, Allah will record for him the same reward as the warrior, without the warrior's reward being diminished at all.'
1695.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Zaid ibn Khalid al-Juhani (radhiyallahu 'anhu) dari Nabi ﷺ yang bersabda: 'Barangsiapa membekali seorang pejuang di jalan Allah atau merawat keluarganya, Allah akan mencatatkan baginya pahala yang sama seperti pejuang, tanpa mengurangi pahala pejuang sedikit pun.'

1696.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Bara' (may Allah be pleased with him) that when this verse was revealed: {Not equal are those believers who sit at home...}, the Messenger of Allah ﷺ called Zayd and he wrote it. Then Ibn Umm Maktum came complaining of his blindness, and Allah revealed: {except those who are disabled} [Quran 4:95].
1696.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Bara' (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa ketika ayat ini diturunkan: {Tidaklah sama orang-orang mukmin yang duduk di rumah...}, Rasulullah ﷺ memanggil Zaid dan memintanya menulisnya. Kemudian Ibnu Ummi Maktum datang mengadukan kebutaannya, maka Allah menurunkan: {kecuali orang-orang yang mempunyai uzur} [QS. An-Nisa: 95].

1697.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas ibn Malik (may Allah be pleased with him) that Umm Haram bint Milhan narrated to him that the Prophet ﷺ said regarding the naval expedition: 'Those aboard this sea are like kings on thrones.' Then Umm Haram asked: O Messenger of Allah, pray for Allah to make me among them. He prayed for her.
1697.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas ibn Malik (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Umm Haram binti Milhan menceritakan kepadanya bahwa Nabi ﷺ bersabda tentang ekspedisi laut: 'Mereka yang berlayar di lautan ini bagaikan raja-raja di atas singgasana.' Kemudian Umm Haram berkata: Wahai Rasulullah, doakan agar Allah menjadikan aku termasuk mereka. Beliau mendoakan untuknya.

1698.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umm Atiyyah (may Allah be pleased with her): 'I campaigned with the Prophet ﷺ seven times, staying at the rear camp, treating the wounded, preparing food for them, and caring for the sick.'
1698.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umm Atiyyah (radhiyallahu 'anha): 'Aku ikut berperang bersama Nabi ﷺ tujuh kali, tinggal di belakang pasukan, merawat yang terluka, menyiapkan makanan untuk mereka, dan merawat yang sakit.'

1699.iwh_en: It was narrated from Aishah (may Allah be pleased with her) that she said: 'When the Messenger of Allah ﷺ intended to travel, he would cast lots among his wives. Whoever's lot came up would travel with him.'
1699.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Aisyah (radhiyallahu 'anha) bahwa dia berkata: 'Ketika Rasulullah ﷺ hendak bepergian, beliau mengundi di antara istri-istrinya. Siapa pun yang undiannya keluar, dia akan ikut bepergian bersamanya.'

1700.iwh_en: It was narrated from Uthman (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said from the pulpit: 'I had withheld from you a hadith I heard from the Messenger of Allah ﷺ, but then feared that if I left it the knowledge would depart: I heard him say: Guarding one day and one night in the cause of Allah is better than fasting and praying for a full month; and if one dies, his good deeds continue running...'
1700.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Utsman (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata dari atas mimbar: 'Aku telah menyembunyikan dari kalian sebuah hadits yang aku dengar dari Rasulullah ﷺ, namun kemudian aku khawatir jika aku tinggalkan maka ilmunya akan pergi: Aku mendengar beliau bersabda: Berjaga sehari dan semalam di jalan Allah lebih baik daripada puasa dan shalat selama satu bulan penuh; dan jika seseorang meninggal, amal baiknya terus mengalir...'

1701.iwh_en: It was narrated from Uqbah ibn Amir (may Allah be pleased with him) that he said: I heard the Messenger of Allah ﷺ say: 'Every dead person has his deeds sealed except the one standing guard at the frontier in the cause of Allah — his deeds continue to grow until the Day of Resurrection, and he is spared the trial of the grave.'
1701.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Uqbah ibn Amir (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa dia berkata: Aku mendengar Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Setiap orang yang meninggal, amalnya terputus kecuali orang yang berjaga di perbatasan di jalan Allah — amalnya terus bertambah hingga Hari Kiamat, dan dia terlindung dari fitnah kubur.'

1702.iwh_en: It was narrated from Urwah al-Bariqi (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Good is tied to the forelocks of horses until the Day of Resurrection: reward and spoils of war.'
1702.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Urwah al-Bariqi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Kebaikan terikat pada ubun-ubun kuda hingga Hari Kiamat: pahala dan rampasan perang.'

1703.iwh_en: It was narrated from Urwah al-Bariqi (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Good is tied to the forelocks of horses until the Day of Resurrection: reward and spoils of war.' He said: I have horses, so pray for blessing in them. He said: 'Then I pray for blessing in your horses.'
1703.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Urwah al-Bariqi (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Kebaikan terikat pada ubun-ubun kuda hingga Hari Kiamat: pahala dan rampasan perang.' Dia berkata: Aku memiliki kuda-kuda, doakan keberkahan untuk mereka. Beliau bersabda: 'Maka aku mendoakan keberkahan untuk kuda-kudamu.'

1704.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Qatadah al-Ansari (may Allah be pleased with him) that a man said: O Messenger of Allah, I want to buy a horse for campaigning — which should I buy? He said: 'Buy the one with the long blazed forehead, four white stockings, and a white patch on its nose — for that is the best kind.'
1704.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Qatadah al-Ansari (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa seorang laki-laki berkata: Wahai Rasulullah, aku ingin membeli kuda untuk berperang — mana yang harus aku beli? Beliau bersabda: 'Belilah yang memiliki dahi berlambang panjang, empat kaki berkaos putih, dan bintik putih di hidungnya — karena itulah yang terbaik.'

1705.iwh_en: It was narrated from Ibn Umar (may Allah be pleased with them) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ would race horses that had been trained from al-Hafya' to al-Thaniyyah, and those that had not been trained from al-Thaniyyah to Masjid Bani Zurayq. Ibn Umar said: I was among those who raced.
1705.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Ibnu Umar (radhiyallahu 'anhuma) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ merlombakan kuda-kuda yang telah dilatih dari al-Hafya' hingga al-Tsaniyyah, dan yang belum dilatih dari al-Tsaniyyah hingga Masjid Bani Zurayq. Ibnu Umar berkata: Aku termasuk orang yang ikut berlomba.

1706.iwh_en: Abu Labid said: 'Horses were raced in the time of al-Hajjaj while al-Hakam ibn Ayyub was over Basra. We attended the race, and when the horses came, we said: let us inquire about the race rules from the Companions of the Messenger of Allah ﷺ — so we asked Anas ibn Malik...'
1706.iwh_id: Abu Labid berkata: 'Kuda-kuda dilombakan pada masa al-Hajjaj sementara al-Hakam ibn Ayyub memimpin Basra. Kami menghadiri perlombaan itu, dan ketika kuda-kuda datang, kami berkata: mari kita tanyakan aturan perlombaan dari para Sahabat Rasulullah ﷺ — maka kami bertanya kepada Anas ibn Malik...'

1707.iwh_en: It was narrated from Anas (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'Fight the polytheists with your wealth, your selves, and your tongues.'
1707.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Anas (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Perangilah orang-orang musyrik dengan harta, jiwa, dan lisan kalian.'

1708.iwh_en: It was narrated from al-Mughirah ibn Shu'bah (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'A group from my nation will always remain victorious over others until the command of Allah comes to them.'
1708.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari al-Mughirah ibn Syu'bah (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Sekelompok orang dari umatku akan senantiasa unggul atas orang lain hingga perintah Allah datang kepada mereka.'

1709.iwh_en: It was narrated from Umar ibn al-Khattab (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'The martyrs are four: a man who believes and is upright in his faith, meets the enemy and is truthful to Allah so he is killed — that one is in the exalted ranks on the Day of Resurrection...'
1709.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Umar ibn al-Khattab (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Para syahid ada empat: seorang laki-laki yang beriman dan teguh dalam imannya, bertemu musuh dan jujur kepada Allah sehingga dia terbunuh — itulah yang ada di peringkat tinggi pada Hari Kiamat...'

1710.iwh_en: It was narrated from Abu Dharr (may Allah be pleased with him) that the Messenger of Allah ﷺ said: 'After me among my nation there will come leaders who will command you with what you do not know and forbid you from what you do know — so whoever faces that should oppose it in his heart, and whoever does so is absolved; but whoever agrees and follows, he is destroyed.'
1710.iwh_id: Diriwayatkan dari Abu Dzar (radhiyallahu 'anhu) bahwa Rasulullah ﷺ bersabda: 'Setelahku di antara umatku akan datang pemimpin-pemimpin yang memerintahkan kalian dengan apa yang tidak kalian ketahui dan melarang kalian dari apa yang kalian ketahui — maka barangsiapa menghadapi hal itu hendaklah dia menolaknya dengan hatinya, dan barangsiapa melakukannya dia terbebas; tetapi barangsiapa setuju dan mengikuti, dia binasa.'
